overview: Golgari Ouroboroid is a midrange strategy that leverages creature deaths and token generation to rapidly pump a central commander. By cycling cheap threats like Badgermole Cub into the graveyard, the deck triggers Ouroboroid's growth while using Nature's Rhythm to maintain card advantage and accelerate the win condition.
win_conditions: Combat damage with a pumped Ouroboroid
Overwhelming the opponent with Badgermole tokens and a large creature
Card advantage via Nature's Rhythm outlasting the opponent

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands
Hands containing Badgermole Cub or Nature's Rhythm
Hands with a playable creature by turn two
Keep hands with at least two lands (preferably duals or fetches if available), Nature's Rhythm, Badgermole Cub, and at least one early creature or removal.
Drop to five if you have a functional two-land start with a key synergistic piece.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking graveyard synergy or card draw
Mulligan hands with three or more lands and no early plays, or hands with only instants/sorceries lacking land support.

early_game: Play lands and cheap creatures like Badgermole Cub
Prioritize drawing cards with Nature's Rhythm
Establish a board presence to fuel Ouroboroid
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Badgermole Cub if mana allows.
Turn 2: Drop a second land, then cast Nature's Rhythm or a 2-drop creature.
Prioritize land drops to enable landfall triggers and set up Nature's Rhythm's value.
Sequence spells to maximize graveyard fuel for Ouroboroid recursion without overcommitting to shuffling prematurely.
mid_game: Sacrifice or trade creatures to grow Ouroboroid
Use Nature's Rhythm to pump and draw simultaneously
Maintain pressure while filtering through the deck
Balance mana between land drops, spell casting, and graveyard manipulation.
Use Nature's Rhythm on yourself when you have 3+ lands and an empty graveyard to stabilize, or on opponents facing graveyard-dependent strategies.
Avoid shuffling your graveyard if you need specific cards for recursion or answers.
Track opponent graveyard size to anticipate their Nature's Rhythm or combo potential.
Commit threats when you have landfall triggers active or sufficient graveyard recursion to replace them.
Hold back against sweepers, heavy removal, or when you lack land synergy to recover.
late_game: Focus all resources into pumping Ouroboroid
Use token generation to block and trade efficiently
Close out the game with combat damage or card advantage
Nature's Rhythm combined with 4+ lands on the battlefield and a healthy graveyard is your primary win condition, often leading to lethal card advantage or overwhelming board presence.
Ouroboroid recursion can close games by repeatedly deploying threats or shuffling key pieces back into play.
Badgermole Cub can dig for the final land or answer needed to secure the game.
Go all-in when you have 3+ lands, a creature on board, and enough graveyard fuel to sustain recursion for at least two turns.
Commit when the opponent has no sweepers or counterspells in hand, or when you can safely cast Nature's Rhythm to draw into game-ending threats.

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.notes: Play early to trigger its death ability, creating a 1/1 token that can also fuel Ouroboroid later.

key_cards.name: Ouroboroid
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and pump target
key_cards.notes: Protect it initially, then feed it creature deaths to make it unblockable and lethal.

key_cards.name: Nature's Rhythm
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn two or three to draw a card and add a counter to Ouroboroid or a token, smoothing out your curve.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Nature's Rhythm for key moments to maximize pump value
Sacrifice Badgermole Cub before trading it to ensure the token triggers
Play Ouroboroid as soon as possible to start accumulating counters
Common mistake: Over-shuffling your graveyard too early, leaving you without recursion fuel.
Remember that Nature's Rhythm targets a player, so use it strategically on yourself for stabilization or on opponents facing graveyard synergy.
Ouroboroid's recursion is your win condition
Practice stack interaction to maximize Nature's Rhythm and Badgermole Cub value.
In Unknown format, always verify specific card rulings and format restrictions before tournament play.

matchups.advice: Play defensively early, use Nature's Rhythm to pump blockers, and race with Ouroboroid once it reaches 4/4.
Apply early pressure with Badgermole Cub and tokens, then pivot to pumping Ouroboroid once they run out of removal.
Race them by maximizing Ouroboroid's growth, using Nature's Rhythm to stay ahead in card advantage.

Play around counters by establishing land advantage first, then casting Nature's Rhythm or creatures in response to their counterspells. Use cheap threats and stack recursion to force them to spend multiple counters. If they have heavy counter suites, prioritize artifact/enchantment removal and graveyard hate to bypass their interaction.

Apply early pressure with Badgermole Cub and early creatures to disrupt their mana development. Use targeted removal on key ramp creatures before they can stabilize. Avoid trading into their late-game finishers; instead, win the resource war by out-graving them and using Nature's Rhythm to draw past their threats.

Win the graveyard war by out-sequenceing your opponent and using counters/removal on key creatures. Prioritize keeping your graveyard stocked for Ouroboroid recursion while stripping theirs. Use Nature's Rhythm to draw into more recursion or answers. Track their land count and graveyard size to anticipate their plays and counter their Nature's Rhythm timing.

common_threats: Watch for Izzet's counterspells and sweepers
they will try to interrupt your landfall or Nature's Rhythm.
Note when opponents shuffle their graveyard—that often signals a big draw or combo setup.
opponents with 4+ lands are primed for massive turns.
